contentDescriptions.description: Represent, compare and order integers and rational numbers, and apply the four operations with integers and decimals.
Solve problems involving fractions, percentages and ratios, including finding a fraction of a quantity.
Introduce and use variables to represent quantities, and construct and evaluate algebraic expressions.
Solve problems involving area, surface area and volume of prisms and cylinders using appropriate units.
Classify triangles and quadrilaterals using properties including side length, angles and parallel sides.
Construct, interpret and compare representations of data including histograms, dot plots and stem-and-leaf plots.
Identify and investigate everyday situations that involve chance, and assign probabilities using fractions, decimals and percentages.
Solve problems involving rates, ratios and proportional reasoning in real-world contexts.
Solve linear equations and inequalities, and represent their solutions on a number line.
Expand and factorise algebraic expressions, and apply index laws with integer exponents.
Apply Pythagoras theorem to find lengths in right-angled triangles in real-world contexts.
Identify and apply geometric transformations including translations, reflections, rotations and dilations.
Apply the real number system including rational and irrational numbers, and use index notation for very large and very small numbers.
Solve simultaneous linear equations using algebraic and graphical methods.
Sketch and interpret graphs of linear and non-linear functions, identifying key features including gradient and intercepts.
Apply trigonometric ratios to solve right-angled triangle problems in practical contexts.
Describe the properties of similar figures and apply them to solve problems involving scale.
Calculate and interpret measures of spread including range, interquartile range and standard deviation.
Construct and compare box plots, back-to-back stem-and-leaf plots and scatter plots to analyse data.
Recognise and apply the connection between simple and compound interest and exponential growth and decay.
Explore and apply quadratic equations and their graphs including parabolas.
Apply the binomial theorem and expand and factorise complex algebraic expressions.
Apply the sine rule and cosine rule to solve problems involving triangles.
Apply deductive reasoning to prove geometric properties and solve complex geometry problems.
